facts: Conquistadors were Spanish explorers who conquered parts of the Americas for gold and for Spain, not to build new homes like settlers did.
English settlers came to North America seeking freedom and a new life, unlike conquistadors who sought gold and conquest.
A colony is a settlement built by people from one country in a new land, and English settlers founded colonies in North America to live there permanently.
Spain and England were both European countries that sent people to the Americas, but Spain sent conquistadors to conquer while England sent settlers to build colonies.
misconceptions: Students think conquistadors and settlers were the same kind of people doing the same thing in the Americas, but conquistadors came to conquer for gold while settlers came to build new homes for freedom.
Students believe all explorers after Columbus were from England, not knowing that Spanish conquistadors also explored and conquered parts of the Americas.
